action.hypothesis: The bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual family has already produced non-dominated gains at K=16,24,32,64,96 under the fixed EF=16 signum recipe; running the same architecture at K=128 isolates the K-axis and tests whether the bucketed locality advantage extends to the high-K anchor, potentially replacing the old routed anchor with a better absolute-FVU backbone.
action.summary: Run a parameter-only follow-up on bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual at K=128 with the same stabilized EF=16 signum recipe to map the high-K endpoint of the newly winning family.
action.self_review: This stays within the one-variable rule by changing only K on the current strongest family. It does not yet satisfy the operator hint to explore optimizer/loss/preprocessing, but it is the cleanest immediate frontier-closing measurement after the K=96 win.
notes_to_memory: Round 130 should close the K-axis map for bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual by testing K=128 under the same EF=16 signum recipe used for the recent K=16..96 wins.
Keep the override set minimal and fully explicit so attribution remains on K alone and avoids recipe drift.
next_hypotheses: If K=128 wins clearly, treat bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual as the new EF=16 backbone from mid-K through the anchor regime.
If K=128 is close but noisy relative to the prior anchor, run one confirmatory repeat before promoting it as default high-K choice.
If K=128 regresses, keep bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual as the low/mid-K winner and pivot the next round to optimizer or loss exploration on an existing high-K architecture.

result.description: Run a parameter-only follow-up on bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual at K=128 with the same stabilized EF=16 signum recipe to map the high-K endpoint of the newly winning family.
result.self_review: This stays within the one-variable rule by changing only K on the current strongest family. It does not yet satisfy the operator hint to explore optimizer/loss/preprocessing, but it is the cleanest immediate frontier-closing measurement after the K=96 win.
